User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Visionary Vault application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

registrationDate.description: Date and time the user registered.

GeneratedContent.title: GeneratedContent
GeneratedContent.description: Represents an AI-generated image or video.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GeneratedContent entity.

generationDate.description: Date and time the content was generated.

aiModelUsed.description: The name of the AI model used to generate the content.

prompt.description: The prompt used to generate the content.

AiModel.title: AiModel
AiModel.description: Represents an AI model used for image or video generation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AI Model entity.

description.description: Description of the AI model's capabilities.

supportedMediaTypes.description: An array of media types supported by this model (e.g., image, video).

PromptExample.title: PromptExample
PromptExample.description: Represents an example prompt to inspire users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PromptExample entity.

description.description: Description of what the prompt generates.

aiModelId.description: Reference to AiModel. The AI model that can be used with this prompt. (Relationship: AiModel 1:N PromptExample)

Collection.title: Collection
Collection.description: Represents a collection of saved generated content for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Collection entity.

generatedContentIds.description: References to GeneratedContent. (Relationship: Collection N:N GeneratedContent)

LikeCount.title: LikeCount
LikeCount.description: Represents the total number of likes for a piece of content.

UserLike.title: UserLike
UserLike.description: A marker to indicate that a user has liked a specific piece of content.

likedAt.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp when the like was created.

PlaceholderImage.title: PlaceholderImage
PlaceholderImage.description: Represents a placeholder image title seeded from a local JSON file.

title.description: The title of the placeholder image.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership enforced by security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated content created by users. Path-based ownership is enforced through the /users/{userId} path, making security rules simple. Each content belongs to one user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who generated the content.

params.name: generatedContentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the generated content.

definition.description: Stores information about available AI models.

params.name: aiModelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the AI model.

definition.description: Stores example prompts for AI models.

params.name: promptExampleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the prompt example.

definition.description: Stores collections of saved generated content for each user. Path-based ownership enforced by security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: collectionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the collection.

definition.description: Stores the like count for each piece of content. Document ID is the content ID.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the content.

definition.description: Tracks which content a user has liked. Path-based ownership is enforced.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique ID of the liked content.

definition.description: Stores placeholder image titles seeded from a script. Publicly readable.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the image.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the Visionary Vault application, emphasizing secure and scalable data management. Key considerations include user-generated content, AI models, prompt examples, and collections of user-saved content. The structure prioritizes authorization independence and clear access control. User-owned data is organized under `/users/{userId}`. The `LikeCount` documents, representing the likes for GeneratedContent, are stored in a top-level collection for easy public access, while `UserLike` documents are stored in a user-specific subcollection to track individual likes securely.

**Authorization Independence:** For collections requiring authorization based on user roles or ownership, the structure denormalizes relevant data. The `GeneratedContent` documents are stored under the user's ID (`/users/{userId}/generatedContent/{generatedContentId}`), implicitly granting the user ownership and simplifying security rules.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure supports secure list operations by segregating data with different access needs. User-generated content is stored under user-specific paths, ensuring that only the content owner can list or modify their content. The use of explicit paths for user-owned data like `/users/{userId}/generatedContent` supports simple and efficient security rules based on the `request.auth.uid` variable, preventing the need for complex filtering in the rules.
